kink.method: hockey-stick LSQ on weekly SOFR-IORB (bp, winsorized 1/99) vs reserves/GDP; grid-searched breakpoint

crunch_windows.reason: $195B auction settlement while reserves sit below the estimated kink
calendar pressure date while reserves sit below the estimated kink
calendar pressure date while reserves sit below the estimated kink

weather.method: reserves + trailing-8wk WALCL drift - seasonal dTGA (day-of-month median buckets, tax/qtr-end aware); bands = 20/80% backtested cumulative error; auction settlement days >= $90B flagged

tails.method: P99-P50 per rate (bp), 250d z, blend SOFR .5/TGCR .3/BGCR .2, EWMA(5)

echo.method: RMSE similarity of 30d z-trajectories vs pre-episode windows (leads 0-30d)

rvxray.method: TFF futures-only; pair=min(levShort,amLong)xface; DV01 per-contract constants in config; scenarios assume 10% forced unwind vs DVP daily volume

crowding.method: leveraged-fund net position / open interest per contract; z and percentile vs trailing 156w; |z| ranks the board (extremes = crowding)

auctions.method: per-tenor z vs trailing history: 0.5(-btc_z)+0.3(pdShare_z)+0.2(-indirect_z); bills x0.35; EWMA(20)

resonance.method: per calendar mode: slosh = max(SOFR−IORB, event ±1bd) − median(10bd baseline ending 3bd prior); amplification = median slosh last 6 events / prior events; decay = days to give back half the slosh (censored 5d). Modes disjoint; weights: quarter_end 0.3, month_end 0.25, year_end 0.15, mid_month 0.15, tax_date 0.15. ex-max = amplification with the largest recent slosh removed (one-event sensitivity); modes with n<10 carry a low-n flag

caveats: indicator series are serially correlated — trends are read as percentiles vs own history, never as p-values
spread/tail belong to the PROOF event's variable family: Undertow is live structural evidence, not an orthogonal predictor
detrended with a rolling median: slow level regimes don't count as damping loss, only the noise dynamics do
mechanism (fluctuation-dissipation split) is a diagnostic label, never weighted into the score
undertow.method: per series: residual = x − rolling 30bd median; AC1 = rolling 120bd lag-1 autocorr; tau = −1/ln(AC1) bd; variance over the same window; both scored as EXPANDING percentiles vs own past. Noise power D = Var·(1−AC1²) (fluctuation-dissipation split), expanding percentile, diagnostic only — AC1 pctl ≥80 names damping loss, D pctl ≥80 names louder forcing. Recovery = median half-life after pops above the expanding 90th pctl (declustered 5bd — an episode is one experiment; censored 10bd; unresolved end-of-sample pops excluded, not censored), trailing year vs prior. Score: AC1 pctl 45 / var pctl 30 / recovery stretch 25; series blend spread 0.6 / tail 0.4, renormalized per row over the series with data

caveats: deterministic lexicon, not comprehension — sarcasm-proof, nuance-blind; word lists frozen in config
statement text only (minutes lag 3 weeks and are out of scope v1)
context engine: narrative is not plumbing evidence — never weighted into the composite
communique.method: per statement: net lexicon hits per 1,000 words (hawkish−dovish, BS-tighten−BS-ease, stress vocabulary); the CHANGE vs the previous statement is the signal; robust-z flags vs the statement's own history

interventions.label: ad-hoc overnight repo operations resume (first since 2008)
QE restart, swap-line enhancement, discount-window cut

breakwater.posture: post-2021 game: the Standing Repo Facility is a goalie who never leaves the net — it caps the tail by construction, so pops cap where pre-SRF pops ran; the live risk is the STIGMA channel (nobody wants to be seen using it first)
breakwater.reading: board 2 percentile points below the revealed rescue threshold
caveats: n=8 interventions — this is a revealed-preference CATALOG, not a fitted model; ranges printed, no point estimate trusted
announcement dating is editorial where flagged; the board replay uses final-vintage data
high proximity cuts both ways: relief becomes likely exactly when the predicted event is real — a forecast miss after an intervention is a SAVE, not a false alarm
context engine: the goalie's likely move is not evidence of stress and never enters the composite
breakwater.method: for each dated plumbing intervention, replay the board as of the day before the announcement (expanding percentile of SOFR−IORB, 20d max spread, 20d max SRF usage); revealed threshold = median pre-intervention spread percentile; rescue proximity = today's percentile mapped 0-100 between the 50th percentile floor and the revealed median

hydrophone.method: absorption ratio = top-2 PC variance share of 120bd standardized daily changes (Kritzman), sampled 5bd; edges = max |lag-k xcorr| k=1..3, |r|>=0.3

instability.verdict: dominant modes are decaying — the basin is damping its oscillations (g* = -0.0902/bd)

forecast_skill.verdict: mode forecast edges persistence out-of-sample (MAE ratio 0.99) — treat as fragile; the modes' value is still the structure, not the point forecast

caveats: DMD is a linear fit to a trailing window of a nonlinear basin — modes are a local linearization, their eigenvalues drift with the window
growth rates near zero flip sign under noise: read g* as an expanding percentile vs the gauge's own history, not as a literal doubling time, until the percentile is extreme
the 5bd mode forecast rarely beats persistence and the verdict self-demotes when it doesn't — the modes are structure evidence, not a trading signal
no composite contribution by doctrine: Merian is context/amplifier evidence, not stress evidence
merian.method: per series: resid = x − trailing 63bd median; z = resid / EXPANDING std (min 120 obs) — trailing only, mixed units enter on equal footing. One Hankel-DMD fit per trailing 250bd window (5 delay embeds; series covering >= 90% of the window, ffill <= 5; SVD rank <= 8 at 90% energy): eigenvalues give period 2π/|arg λ| bd and growth ln|λ| per bd; amplitudes solved against the LAST snapshot (current excitation); conjugate pairs collapsed. Instability g* = max growth over modes with period in 4–84bd (or non-oscillatory) and amp share >= 5%, sampled every 5bd walk-forward at fixed offsets, read as an expanding percentile vs own history. Forecast honesty: modes propagated 5bd, MAE vs persistence, published either way. A high-amplitude ~21bd mode, when present, is Resonance's month-end forcing seen a second, independent way.

roguewave.tail_verdict: heavy-tailed basin (xi=0.64): the largest wave in the sample is NOT the largest the basin can make
caveats: declustering keeps cluster MAXIMA, deliberately unlike PROOF's first-day convention: PROOF wants lead time (the first day of the run), EVT wants magnitudes (the biggest wave of the episode) — same water, different question
bootstrap CIs resample the 61 excesses with lambda_u held FIXED — threshold-choice and exceedance-rate uncertainty are not propagated, so the intervals are if anything too narrow
a tail fit on n=61 cluster maxima is an extrapolation with stated error bars, not a measurement — read the CIs before the points
PWM is consistent only for xi < 0.5: a fit near that edge means 'very heavy', not a precise number

expanding statistics only: the current fit uses ALL history up to now; the xi history refits at fixed integer positions (500 rows, then every 252) so no published value changes when future data arrives
no composite score: the tail law is context about the basin, not evidence of stress today (doctrine)
roguewave.method: pop = SOFR−IORB − trailing 5bd median (imported from backtest.pop_bp, THE shared event statistic; n=2059bd). Peaks-over-threshold: u = 80th pctl of the positive pops = 5.0bp; runs declustering (gap > 5bd starts a new cluster, cluster maximum kept) -> n=61 excesses at lambda_u = 61/2059 clusters/bd. GPD(xi, beta) by probability-weighted moments (Hosking–Wallis 1987). T-year return level = u + (beta/xi)((252·T·lambda_u)^xi − 1); P(pop ≥ x within h bd) = 1 − exp(−h·lambda_u·S(x)) above u, empirical per-bd rate below u. CIs: 400 bootstrap resamples of the excess vector, seed 7, lambda_u fixed. Return levels extend P(pop ≥ x) beyond the sample maximum (305.0bp) with stated uncertainty — the number Swell cannot produce.

warehouse.method: NY Fed PD stats: net outright UST positions summed over bills + coupon buckets ($B, weekly, published T+9); saturation = percentile vs full spliced history; long-end = share in >7y buckets

basins.anchor: SONIA level (no keyless daily policy anchor)
INR/USD (FX channel only — rates feed pending)

basins.out_of_scope: Japan (TONA), China, Russia, African markets: no keyless daily feed that meets the provenance bar — excluded rather than faked. India rides the FX channel only (CCIL = HTML, RBI DBIE = broken SSL, probed 2026-07-07); a rates anchor joins when a qualifying feed exists
basins.method: tide = top-2 PC variance share of 120bd standardized daily changes across basins+channels (sampled 5bd, pctl vs own history); edges = max |lag-k xcorr| k=1..3, |r|>=0.25; confession = NY Fed USD swap ops ex small-value tests + H.4.1 outstanding

TGA.note: innovations vs the fiscal seasonal model — burns = unscheduled cash moves (debt-ceiling games, buyback shifts)

RRP.note: burns = the shock absorber refilling/draining outside its calendar rhythm

WALCL.note: burns = balance-sheet policy changes (QT stop, RMP pace) vs the trailing 26w drift

stationkeeping.method: orbit-determination transfer: propagate expected state (fiscal seasonal / calendar-bucket / trailing drift), standardize one-step innovations by rolling MAD (floor $0.5B), two-sided CUSUM k=0.5, h=6.0 daily / 4.0 weekly; alarm = unmodeled burn. Doubles as the Weather model's health monitor. Context, not composite.

status.note: ACCRUING — 5/250 daily obs; context only until then (never in the composite, never a model feature)

farbasin.why: censorship intensity is a confession channel: what a state rushes to delete is policy fear readable before it becomes a market print — no market data vendor carries it
farbasin.method: DDTI deletion-threat (daily max of 3h prints), newly-targeted term count, and the Generative Firewall Index from palimpsest.info CI-published readings (GitHub raw mirror fallback); history accrues locally on every fetch; quarantined from all models until 250 daily obs

moorings.caveat: USDC/DAI pegs are spot values (no free keyless history); USDT peg history is Coinbase daily closes; context for the dollar system, not weighted into the Seiche Index
moorings.method: peg dev bp vs $1 (flag |dev| ≥ 30.0bp); USDT |dev| robust-z 250d; offshore demand = Δ total circulation (drain flag ≤ -3.0%/30d); canary = BTC 10d realized vol z + largest weekend move (crypto trades when funding markets sleep)

movers.name: WRESBAL
movers.label: Reserve balances with Federal Reserve Banks

sonar.method: robust z = (last − median) / (1.4826·MAD) over trailing 250 obs, on level and 1d change; flag |z| ≥ 2.5

history.method: Seiche-lite: expanding-window standardization only (no look-ahead); components tails/kink-proxy/confession/rvxray/auctions/buffers with live composite weights renormalized; weather/resonance/hydrophone/warehouse are live-only and excluded (stated, not hidden); final-vintage data

tell.method: Tell = rolling-750d percentile of Seiche-lite minus weighted rolling percentiles of VIX/HY OAS/IG OAS/10y realized vol (weights {'VIX': 0.35, 'HY_OAS': 0.3, 'IG_OAS': 0.15, 'RATES_VOL': 0.2}); divergence is a signal, not stress evidence

playbook.caveat: historical distributions conditioned on the current state; overlapping forward windows mean n_days overstates independent samples (n_independent ≈ n_days/horizon shown); native units; not investment advice
playbook.method: state = Seiche-lite regime × Tell bucket; forward outcomes over [5, 20] bd on matching historical days

validation.note: model skill vs naive is only 0.027 on LOO-CV — statistically indistinguishable from naive, so the naive same-mode median is published

turn.method: OLS on 7 features frozen T-5bd before each historical turn; LOO-CV vs naive same-mode trailing median; band = LOO residual 20/80%

event_capture.precision_note: day-level; alert days are serially correlated — use run-level

caveats: expanding-window standardization only — no look-ahead in the signal
alert threshold fixed in config, not fitted to outcomes
final-vintage data (weekly H.4.1 aggregates are lightly revised; daily market prints effectively are not)
overlapping forward windows: n_independent ≈ n_days / horizon is shown
Seiche-lite excludes live-only engines (weather/resonance/hydrophone/warehouse) — the live index has MORE information than this backtest
event count is small — Wilson 95% intervals are printed next to every rate; run-level precision is the load-bearing number (alert days are serially correlated)
the lite index contains spread/tail terms and the event is a spread spike — see the ORTHOGONAL test for the same claim with the target's own variables removed
backtest.method: event = SOFR−IORB ≥ +10.0bp vs trailing 5d median (clusters collapsed); alert = Seiche-lite expanding pctl ≥ 80.0; capture window 5bd

event_capture.precision_note: day-level; alert days are serially correlated — use run-level

orthogonal.why: same event-capture test with the target's own variable family removed from the signal (no spread, no tails) — kink-proxy/confession/rvxray/auctions/buffers only

analogs.episode: T-40d before Apr 2025 tariff shock basis unwind
T-13d before Sep 2025 tax-date squeeze (SOFR +18bp over EFFR)

skill.verdict: analogs do NOT beat climatology on this sample — read the fan as context, trust the base rate

caveats: k analogs overlap in market regimes even after de-clustering — the CI understates true uncertainty
expanding-z state — early-sample analogs are scored on less-settled statistics
novelty well charted: nearest analogs are genuinely close
tidetables.method: state = expanding-z of 8 plumbing series; query = trailing 20bd trajectory; analogs = 25 nearest RMSE matches over all history, min 10bd apart, ending >= 20bd before today (no shared days, forward outcomes closed); fan = percentiles of analog forward spread paths (10bd); event odds = share of analogs with a funding event (PROOF definition) within 5bd, vs candidate base rate; novelty = NN-distance pctl vs each day's own past; skill = walk-forward Brier/AUROC vs climatology

validation.verdict: curve beats climatology out-of-sample — the dates AND the levels carry information

caveats: forward days condition on TODAY'S damping state held constant — state drift over the horizon is not modeled (stated, not hidden)
multi-week horizons compound daily rates as if pop-days were independent; pops CLUSTER, so h≥10 cumulative numbers are upper bounds — only the 5bd integral is walk-forward validated
year turns see ~2 obs/year by construction — their rates borrow strength from quarter-turn evidence via shrinkage (low-n flagged), Wilson CIs printed on the raw counts
check the reliability table before reading any probability as literal odds; the verdict self-demotes on levels
replay note: the announced-settlement overlay has no historical vintage — Time Machine replays run calendar-only
swell.method: pop = SOFR−IORB − trailing 5bd median (the PROOF event statistic; event = pop ≥ 10bp). Each bd belongs to one forcing bucket; P(pop ≥ x | bucket) = expanding empirical exceedance with empirical-Bayes shrinkage toward the parent bucket (year→quarter→month turn, tax→mid-month, all→pooled; prior weight 20) — a 2-obs-per-year bucket borrows strength instead of diluting to a plain day. Severity monotonicity enforced after lifts. Damping-state lift (Undertow pctl ≥ 67) and coupon-settlement lift (≥ $90B on mid-month/plain days) estimated expanding, capped (0.5, 3.0). Curve compounds day probabilities; validation = walk-forward P(event within 5bd) vs climatology (AUROC/Brier/reliability)

validation.verdict: the fitted dynamics do NOT beat climatology on levels — read the landscape (well, barrier, gap) as structure, not the probabilities as odds

caveats: x is the PROOF event's own variable family — a forecast layer and dynamics diagnosis, never composite evidence
Markov(1) on binned daily states: longer memory, intraday structure and the calendar are not modeled (the calendar is Swell's job — disagreement between the two is itself information)
forward probabilities and the MFPT hold today's operator frozen over the horizon

the Fokker–Planck ↔ Schrödinger mapping is exact only under detailed balance; the measured irreversibility (the arrow) says how far from that the basin runs, and the spectrum is read on eigenvalue moduli
bin edges are fixed editorial constants — data-dependent bins would leak the future into the past
bathymetry.method: state x = SOFR−IORB pop (the shared PROOF statistic), binned [-8, 10)bp in 1bp steps + one event bin ≥ 10bp; expanding transition counts shrunk toward the pooled increment kernel (K=15). Floor: Kramers–Moyal D1/D2 per bin, V = −∫D1 dx, barrier printed in units of the well's diffusion (k_BT). Spectrum: E_k = −ln|λ_k| of the propagator restricted to visited bins (no evidence, no eigenvalue), τ = 1/gap. Arrow: Schnakenberg entropy production of the same chain. Escape: absorbing-boundary first passage, P(event ≤ h bd) and MFPT, walk-forward validated vs climatology (warmup 500 transitions)

live.verdict: CHOP — this pop reads as calendar mechanics

caveats: pops are declustered (>7 calendar days apart) so trials are ~independent — but regimes still overlap
the model is a 4-feature logistic on purpose: a few hundred pops cannot discipline more
a pop with its expected RRP co-move is choreography; without it, scarcity — the discriminator the 2025 squeezes proved
riptide.method: pop = shared PROOF statistic ≥ 4bp, declustered; targets: STICKY = half-give-back time ≥ 3bd (window 15bd), ESCALATES = ≥10bp event within 10bd; features as-of pop-day close (pop_bp, rrp_co_z, is_turn, damping_pctl); expanding walk-forward logistic across pops after 30 warmup; validated pop-by-pop vs the base rate

nonlinearity.verdict: state-dependent (nonlinear) dynamics — the basin obeys different rules at different states

stability.verdict: locally contracting (lambda 0.80) — perturbations near today's state damp out

forecast.verdict: simplex beats persistence at 5bd (MAE ratio 0.92) and the determinism gate passes — the neighbor fan carries real skill

caveats: Tide Tables asks WHICH history rhymes; the Gyre asks whether the dynamics are deterministic enough to rhyme at all — a failed determinism gate demotes every analog forecast to linear skill
Bathymetry reconstructs the STOCHASTIC equation of motion (drift + noise) for the same basin; the Gyre asks the complementary deterministic question — both can be right at once
residual family = Undertow's residual (spread minus rolling median), so this shares the PROOF event's variable family: predictability evidence, never composite stress evidence
daily closes only — the intraday dynamics where much of the funding action lives are invisible at this sampling
E was chosen once on the warmup segment and frozen — later data never re-votes the embedding, which is what makes the choice truncation-stable
gyre.method: resid = spread − rolling 30bd median (Undertow's residual family), scaled by EXPANDING MAD (min 120 obs, trailing) → x. Takens delay embedding v_t = (x_t,…,x_(t−E+1)); E scanned 2–8 at h=1 with every target, library vector and realized future strictly inside the first 500 rows, then FROZEN — the choice is truncation-stable because later data never re-votes it. Simplex projection: k=E+1 nearest library neighbors, library = vectors whose h-step future resolved strictly before t with Theiler exclusion |s−t| ≥ E+h (min 300 vectors), weights exp(−d/d_min). Decay: Pearson rho and MAE-vs-persistence per h in (1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21) over post-warmup targets. Determinism gate: 20 phase-randomized surrogates (rfft phases scrambled, DC/Nyquist kept, seed 7) preserve the linear autocorrelation exactly; actual rho must clear their 95th percentile (targets subsampled every 3, same subsample for the actual). S-map: locally weighted linear regression over the full library, weights exp(−theta·d/d̄), theta in (0.0, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 4.0, 8.0), lstsq on the weighted design (every 5th target); state dependence = rho_best − rho(0) > 0.03. Stability: the S-map coefficients at theta 2 form the top row of an E×E companion matrix; largest |eigenvalue| = local expansion multiplier, read as an expanding percentile vs its own past. Live forecast: simplex h=5 from the latest v_t, neighbor-future p25/p75, rescaled to residual bp by the last expanding MAD; verdict gated on BOTH MAE ratio < 1 at h=5 and the determinism gate

ml.verdict: does not out-rank the rule-based index (0.817 vs 0.849); probability levels beat climatology

utility.unit: net caught-events per year (false alarms charged at cost)

caveats: events are rare — AUROC on few positives deserves humility
walk-forward only; no shuffled CV (leakage on time series)
5bd boundary embargo: training rows whose labels see into the test block are dropped
features trailing-only; labels final-vintage (same caveat as PROOF)
probabilities are raw model outputs; check the reliability table before trusting a level
ml.method: HistGradientBoosting (depth 3), expanding walk-forward refits every 42bd after 500d warmup, 5bd embargo; target = funding event (+10.0bp vs 5d median) within 5bd

transfer.verdict: TED-era pretraining helps out-of-sample (+0.007 AUROC) — the funding-stress grammar generalizes across eras

orthogonal.verdict: AUROC 0.787 (no rule comparator in this feature set); probability LEVELS do not beat climatology — use for ranking/alerting, not as literal odds

utility.unit: net caught-events per year (false alarms charged at cost)

stacker.verdict: published signal = mean (Brier 0.0409 vs mean 0.0409, climatology 0.0399); does NOT beat the best single member (swell) — the ensemble adds robustness, not skill
caveats: members share upstream data — dispersion understates true model disagreement
~6 historical episodes: the stack is capped at ~10 parameters on purpose
walk-forward with boundary embargo; members are themselves walk-forward outputs (no peeking anywhere in the chain)
missing members imputed with train-window means; per-member coverage published
stacker.method: per-member 1-D logistic calibration + logistic stack over calibrated members and regime dummies; expanding walk-forward (warmup 250d, refit 42bd, embargo 5bd); published = stack only if it beats the equal-weight mean OOS; target = PROOF funding event within 5bd

today.rationale: P(event,5bd)=0.07 vs enter≥0.35/exit<0.22; fleet dispersion 0.04; Tell +49

backtest.verdict: the Book does NOT beat the static mix after costs (net Sharpe 0.31 CI [-0.27, 1.0] vs 0.63); beats all-cash (4.69% vs 2.9%/yr); does NOT survive doubled transaction costs

live.note: live record starts accruing with the first published book

book.duration_note: duration sleeves are PAPER PROXIES: r ≈ y/252 − D·Δy + ½C·Δy² on constant-maturity par yields (D/C fixed in config), not tradeable futures — no roll-down, no CTD switches, no financing spread; validated for sign and rough magnitude only
caveats: PAPER P&L on proxy returns — see duration_note; no borrow/financing beyond the cash leg; close-to-close only
the stance map was frozen knowing the history this backtest reuses — the live hash-chained record is the only arbiter free of that critique
persistent positions: Sharpe CI is stationary-block-bootstrap (21bd) and the Newey–West t-stat is printed; n_stance_runs is the independent-ish n
BTC weekend moves fold into Monday on the business-day grid
not investment advice
book.method: stance = hysteresis on ensemble P (enter 0.35/exit 0.22; risk_on P≤0.08 & Tell≤-15.0) + dispersion gate 0.25; per-sleeve vol targeting 10% ann (lookback 63d), |w|≤1.0, gross≤2.0; signal t → returns t+1; costs per config; benchmarks through the identical pipeline
